elements.sent-2411-001: This paper introduces the class of cooperative games with generalized coalition configuration.
elements.sent-2411-002: This new class of games corresponds to cooperative games with coalition configuration and restricted cooperation.
elements.sent-2411-003: A coalition configuration is a collection of coalitions covering the agent set.
elements.sent-2411-004: The restriction of cooperation between agents is represented by a set system on each element of the coalition configuration.
elements.sent-2411-005: A coalition profile is a list of feasible coalitions, one for each element of the coalition configuration.
elements.sent-2411-006: A coalition profile function associates a worth with each coalition profile.
elements.sent-2411-007: Based on this framework, we define and axiomatically characterize marginal values whose coefficients induce a unitary flow on the product digraph obtained from these set systems.
elements.sent-2411-008: Next, we propose a two-step procedure, inspired by Owen's procedure, to construct flow methods as above.
elements.sent-2411-009: Then, we show that the associated flow is decomposable into two flows.
elements.sent-2411-010: Finally, we use two axioms to characterize the flows that can be decomposed in this way, and hence the flow methods constructed using our procedure.
elements.sent-2411-011: Given a finite set of agents, a coalition configuration is a collection of coalitions on that set such that the union of these coalitions is equal to this set of agents (Albizuri et al. \cite{Albizuri2006a ).}
elements.sent-2411-012: We consider cooperative games with coalition configuration, with the novelty that for each element of the coalition configuration, a set system represents the set of coalitions that can be formed within this element, thus generalizing coalition configuration structures considered so far in the literature.
elements.sent-2411-013: These structures are referred to as generalized coalition configurations.
elements.sent-2411-014: In this setting, a coalition profile is defined as a list of feasible coalitions, one for each element of the coalition configuration.
elements.sent-2411-015: This profile represents the way in which the population organizes itself to cooperate in each element of the coalition configuration.
elements.sent-2411-016: The coalition profile function associates a worth to each feasible coalition profile.
elements.sent-2411-017: A value for these cooperative games with generalized coalition configuration is a function that associates a payoff vector to each game of this class.
elements.sent-2411-018: As a first step, we consider values that satisfy three principles inspired by the axioms for cooperative games with transferable utility (in short TU-games): Efficiency, Linearity and the Null agent principle.
elements.sent-2411-019: We show that these three principles induce marginalist values whose coefficients lead to a unitary flow on the product digraph constructed from the cartesian product of the covering relations of the set systems (Theorem \ref{th:marg_cov and Theorem \ref{flow-Eff ).}}
elements.sent-2411-020: Such a value is called a flow method.
elements.sent-2411-021: Theorem \ref{th:marg_cov and Theorem \ref{flow-Eff extend Theorem 2.4 and Theorem 2.10 in Aguilera et al. \cite{Aguilera2010 obtained for TU-games with restricted cooperation.}}}
elements.sent-2411-022: Flow methods are allocation methods popularized by Weber \cite{Weber1988 for TU-games and by Moulin and Vohra \cite{Moulin-2003 for cost-sharing problems.}}
elements.sent-2411-023: In a second step, we construct Owen-type values \cite{Owen1977 using a two-step procedure.}
elements.sent-2411-024: In the first step of the procedure, the elements of the coalition configuration play a game with no restrictions on cooperation.
elements.sent-2411-025: In this step, a flow method is applied.
elements.sent-2411-026: The flow passes through the edges of the directed hypercube formed by all the coalition profiles that can be formed between the elements of the coalition configuration.
elements.sent-2411-027: In a second step of the procedure, the agents of each element of the coalition configuration play a cooperative game that takes into account both the cooperation restrictions in that element of the coalition configuration and the result of the previous step.
elements.sent-2411-028: A flow method is applied at this step of the procedure.
elements.sent-2411-029: The flow passes through the edges of the directed graph associated with the covering relation of the set system of this element of the coalition configuration.
elements.sent-2411-031: Firstly, Theorem \ref{char-flowmethod shows that Owen-type values obtained by this procedure are flow methods, called two-step flow methods.}
elements.sent-2411-032: The flow goes through the directed edges on the product digraph defined above.
elements.sent-2411-033: Moreover, this flow can be decomposed into two flows.
elements.sent-2411-034: More precisely, it can be calculated as the product of the flow resulting from the first step of the procedure and the flow resulting from the second step of the procedure.
elements.sent-2411-035: Among the family of such two-step flow methods are those based on maximal paths, in the spirit of Shapley's procedure \cite{Shapley1953 , where the grand coalition is formed step by step from a linear order on the agents set.}
elements.sent-2411-036: With the same idea Owen \cite{Owen1977 and Albizuri et al. \cite{Albizuri2006a construct their value from certain maximal paths and unitary flows on these paths.}}
elements.sent-2411-037: This allows us to extend the configuration value of Albizuri et al. \cite{Albizuri2006a {\footnote{Albizuri and Aurrekoetxea \cite{Albizuri2006b also provide a generalization of the normalized Banzhaf-Coleman index (Dubey and Shapley \cite{DS1979 ) in the setting of cooperative games with coalition configuration.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-038: Likewise, Albizuri and Vidal-Puga \cite{Albizuri2015 study weighted bounded configuration values.}
elements.sent-2411-039: A second result focuses on the characterization of two-step flow methods among the flow methods.
elements.sent-2411-040: Theorem \ref{char-twostepflow1 and Theorem \ref{char-twostepflow provide an axiomatic characterization of two-step flow methods using two axioms about flows.}}
elements.sent-2411-041: The first axiom states that certain directed edges of the product digraph receive no flows.
elements.sent-2411-042: The second axiom is a principle of flow proportionality between certain directed edges of the product digraph.
elements.sent-2411-043: Next, we return to the situation where cooperation among the agents is not restricted.
elements.sent-2411-044: Among the flow methods, we point out a configuration-type value for our situation where the unit of cooperation are not coalitions but coalition profiles.
elements.sent-2411-045: Theorem \ref{AZ-theo provides an axiomatic characterization of this configuration-type value among the flow methods in terms of two axioms of anonymity for flows and the axiom defined above concerning null flow on certain directed edges of the product digraph.}
elements.sent-2411-046: Finally, we explore the possibility of moving from coalition profiles to coalitions.
elements.sent-2411-047: More precisely, we address the question of the conditions under which a flow method defined for situations where the unit of cooperation is a coalition profile induces a flow method for situations where the unit of cooperation is the coalition calculated from the union of the coalitions in a coalition profile.
elements.sent-2411-048: Theorem \ref{final provides such a condition on the product digraph, which is satisfied if the coalition configuration is a partition of the agent set or if each set system is regular.}
elements.sent-2411-049: It should be noted that by flow method here, we mean a marginalist and efficient value whose coefficients define a flow on a digraph built from the set of feasible coalitions obtained from the feasible coalition profiles.
elements.sent-2411-050: This digraph does not necessarily coincide with the digraph of the covering relation over the set of feasible coalitions, but it is consistent with the underlying product digraph.
elements.sent-2411-051: Nervertheless, it coincides with the digraph of the covering relation in the two cases above: the coalition configuration is a partition of the agent set or if each set system is regular.
elements.sent-2411-052: The rest of the article is organized as follows.
elements.sent-2411-053: Section \ref{Prelim provides general definitions about set systems, digraphs and flows.}
elements.sent-2411-054: Section \ref{Section-Games introduces cooperative games with generalized coalition configuration, i.e., cooperative games with coalition configuration and restricted cooperation.}
elements.sent-2411-055: Section \ref{Sec-Marg introduces marginalist values and flow methods for this class of cooperative games.}
elements.sent-2411-056: Section \ref{Sec-Marg-Charact provides an axiomatic characterization of the marginalist values and flow methods.}
elements.sent-2411-057: Section \ref{Section-Two-step-FM introduces and characterizes two-step flow methods.}
elements.sent-2411-058: Section \ref{Section-Two-step-FM-Chara proposes two axioms for flows and shows that these axioms single out the flow of the two-step flow methods.}
elements.sent-2411-059: Section \ref{coalitionp-coalition discusses the conditions under which a flow method for games where the relevant unit of cooperation is a coalition profile induces a flow method for games where the relevant unit of cooperation is reduced to a coalition.. Section \ref{concl concludes.}}

elements.section-2411-003: Cooperative games with generalized coalition configuration
elements.sent-2411-079: Let $N = \{1, 2, \ldots, n \}$ be a finite agent set of size $n \in \mathbb{N}$.
elements.sent-2411-080: Every subset of $N$ is called a {\bf coalition .}
elements.sent-2411-081: Following Albizuri et al. \cite{Albizuri2006a , a {\bf coalition configuration is a list $\mathcal {P = (P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_m)$, $m\geq 1$, of non-empty coalitions that cover $N$, i.e., \begin{equation}\bigcup_{q = 1}^{m} P_q = N.\end{equation}.}}}
elements.sent-2411-082: These structures generalizes the so-called coalition structures introduced by Aumann and Dr\`eze \cite{AD1974 .}
elements.sent-2411-083: A {\bf coalition structure is a coalition configuration $\mathcal {P}$ that forms a partition of $N$.}
elements.sent-2411-084: However, the assumption of full cooperation within each coalition of a coalition configuration is not always realistic, as there may be asymmetries or incompatibilities between agents in each coalition of a coalition configuration.
elements.sent-2411-085: This is why we introduce the notion of {\bf generalized coalition configuration which takes into account the possibilities of restricted cooperation in each element of the coalition configuration.}
elements.sent-2411-086: Hence, denote by $M = \{1, \ldots, m \}$ the index set whose elements label the elements of $\mathcal {P}$.
elements.sent-2411-087: For each $q \in M$, $(P_q, {\cal F _q)$ represents a normal set system.}
elements.sent-2411-088: Each element of ${\cal F _q$ is a {\bf feasible coalition of $P_q$.}}

elements.sent-2411-230: When agents are arranged in a coalition configuration, the payoff allocation procedure can be designed as a two-step procedure.
elements.sent-2411-231: In the first step, the procedure settles the payoff allocation between the elements of the coalition configuration.
elements.sent-2411-232: In the second step, the procedure settles the payoff allocation between the agents in each element of the coalition configuration, taking into account the result of the first step.
elements.sent-2411-233: In the particular case where the coalition configuration ${\cal P}$ is a partition of the agent set $N$ and the set function $v$ is defined over $2^N$, that is $(N, v)$ is a TU-game without restricted cooperation, Owen \cite{Owen1977 proposes such a procedure by applying the Shapley value (Shapley \cite{Shapley1953 ) at each step.}}
elements.sent-2411-234: The resulting value is known as the Owen value.

elements.sent-2411-505: We introduce generalized coalition configurations in which the cooperation in each element of the coalition configuration is not necessarily total but takes into account the possible incompatibilities among the agents.
elements.sent-2411-506: This leads to a variety of novel situations depending on the particular set system in each element of the coalition configuration.
elements.sent-2411-507: First, we provide an axiomatic characterization of flow methods for cooperative games with generalized coalition configuration.
elements.sent-2411-508: Next, we look at a value construction procedure inspired by Owen's two-step procedure for TU-games with coalition structure (Owen \cite{Owen1977 ).}
elements.sent-2411-509: This two-step procedure leads to a flow method whose flow can be decomposed into two flows.
elements.sent-2411-510: Such decomposable flows are characterized by two axioms for flows.
elements.sent-2411-511: Finally, we investigate the possibility of deriving a flow method for TU-games with generalized coalition configuration from a flow method for our class of cooperative games where the unit of cooperation is a coalition profile and not a coalition.. {\bf Acknowledgements.}
